title: Project structure definition
description: Define and document the repository layout for the Fibonacci exploration project.
includes: location of the main instructional markdown file
optional assets directory for future visual artifacts
purpose: Ensure file management is clear and responsibilities are separated from the start.

title: Instructional markdown outline
description: Create the full section outline for the final Fibonacci exploration markdown document.
purpose: Provide a complete teaching structure before implementation details are written.

title: Recursive Fibonacci implementation unit
description: Create a dedicated unit responsible only for naive recursive Fibonacci calculation.
purpose: Keep recursive logic isolated, readable, and testable.

title: Iterative Fibonacci implementation unit
description: Create a dedicated unit responsible only for iterative Fibonacci calculation.
purpose: Keep iterative logic isolated, efficient, and testable.

title: Shared input validation approach
description: Define how input validation will be handled so both strategies behave consistently.
purpose: Avoid duplicated validation logic and inconsistent error handling.

title: Strategy abstraction decision
description: Define the abstraction boundary for comparing multiple Fibonacci implementations.
includes: decision on function-based or class-based design
rules for extending the design later
purpose: Support clean growth without overengineering the initial solution.

title: Comparison data preparation support
description: Create a focused unit that prepares side-by-side comparison data for recursive and iterative approaches.
purpose: Separate comparison assembly from the core calculation logic.

title: Big O analysis content
description: Write the algorithm-complexity explanation for recursive and iterative Fibonacci.
purpose: Ensure the project explains not just how the approaches work, but how they scale.

title: Visualization data preparation support
description: Create a focused unit that prepares data for visualizing Fibonacci growth and algorithm cost growth.
includes: separation between sequence values and computational effort
purpose: Support clear visual storytelling without mixing it into calculation functions.

title: Markdown-friendly visualizations
description: Add visual representations that work directly in markdown without requiring graphics code in the first version.
includes: ASCII bars or similar text visuals
purpose: Make growth patterns and tradeoffs visible in a classroom-friendly format.

title: Design decision notes
description: Document the reasoning behind file management, abstraction choices, and separation of responsibilities.
includes: why recursive and iterative logic are separate
purpose: Connect software design choices to maintainability and clarity.

title: Unit test suite
description: Create a unit test set that covers the main Fibonacci logic and supporting helpers.
includes: matching-result tests for recursive and iterative approaches
invalid-input tests if validation is implemented
tests for comparison or visualization helper behavior
purpose: Verify correctness and protect the separation of responsibilities with focused tests.

title: Testing strategy documentation
description: Document what is being tested, why it is being tested, and how tests align with clean design.
includes: guidance on avoiding tests tied too closely to formatting
purpose: Make the testing approach understandable to learners and future maintainers.

title: Clean code review pass
description: Perform a focused review for naming, function size, duplication, and module clarity.
purpose: Ensure the final work demonstrates the coding standards described in the plan.

title: Final Fibonacci exploration document
description: Produce the finished markdown artifact that combines explanation, comparison, analysis, visualization, and design notes.
purpose: Deliver the complete educational and software-design-focused exploration.

title: Planned commit sequence
description: Maintain an intentional commit plan that reflects the staged delivery of the work.
purpose: Support a clean development history that matches the deliverable sequence.
